concerns.text: The plan relies on a single verified HuggingFace dataset (`GFA-D2/pilot_flags`) for training, while the spec (FR-001, US-1) mandates merging 'Dataset-001' and 'Dataset-002 (Materials Project)'. The research.md explicitly states 'No verified URL exists' for Materials Project and proposes a fallback to the single dataset. This creates a methodology failure: the model will be trained on a subset of the required data scope defined in the spec, potentially lacking the diversity or volume needed to generalize to the 30-element ternary screening space. The plan must either identify a verified alternative source for the Materials Project data or explicitly revise the spec to remove the requirement for Dataset-002.

FR-001 cites 'Dataset-001 (2014 Experimental GFA, source:)' with a missing URL. While the upstream resolver guarantees reachability for *cited* URLs, this requirement text is incomplete and references a non-existent source string. The plan (research.md) attempts to resolve this by mapping to a specific HuggingFace URL, but the spec itself contains a broken citation. This indicates a mismatch between the spec's data requirements and the available verified resources, risking implementation failure if the '2014 Experimental GFA' dataset is not the one hosted at the HuggingFace URL provided in research.md.
